rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GENETTE TRAMMELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Return Of Estate Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/24/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. Although file stamped on 8/17/23, the second amended petition was not actually e-filed until 8/21/23, when it was first available for viewing on the court's website. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (2) A petition pursuant to Probate Code Section 850 requires 30 days' notice. (3) A pleading form of proof of service is inadequate unless a copy of the notice of hearing that was served is attached to it, which mandatory Judicial Council form of notice shows all of the pertinent case and hearing information. (4) The service list attached to the proof of service filed on 8/17/23 does not include all of the parties identified in the pleading. (5) Notice must be personally served on each person having possession of the property. [Probate Code Section 851(a)(2).] (6) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (7) The intended beneficiary of the testamentary SNT survived the testator but died before this estate could close. Has a personal representative been represented for his estate or did he have his own separate trust? (Who is his successor-in-interest?) (8) The remainder beneficiaries of the (future) testamentary SNT are the testatorâs children living at the time of Donald Love Trammell's death. Patricia Ann Trammell was listed in the initial Petition for Probate as a daughter, but she was not given notice. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 10/13/23. (It would be helpful if the second amended petition were supplemented to list all of the parties with their correct, current addresses.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF WILLIAM ESTRADA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE CONSERVATORSHIP of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/18/23. (No earlier availability.) The accounting petition is continued to be heard after the petition filed by Laura Brockwehl is heard. Additionally, the following issues must be addressed prior to 10/18/23: (1) The accounting is not a proper court-conformed accounting. Whether or not the Judicial Council forms are used by a standard account filer, the account must state the date of any order authorizing payment of fiduciary or attorney fees. [CRC 7.575(e)(6)(C).] See Judicial Council forms GC-400(PH)(1) and GC-400(C)(4). There is no schedule of assets on hand either. (2) Inexplicably, separate receipts and disbursements schedules were prepared for each financial account. Since separate accountings were prepared for each separate financial account, there should be a summary of account for each separate financial account; one summary of account is not sufficient when there are not even subtotals shown on the supporting schedules. (3) The Schwab accounts, appraised by the conservator as "cash," clearly did not consist of just cash. For example, "qualified dividends" must refer to holdings other than cash, and "change in value of investment" cannot be true for cash. Securities must be appraised by a probate referee and changes in the form of assets, such as reinvested dividends, must be shown on an informational schedule. (4) If there were sales of securities, they must be shown on separate gains and losses schedules, not on a disbursements schedule. (5) The I&A filed on 8/4/21 was missing the date of appointment of the conservator and revised a portion of item 3 with language that does not make sense. (6) Photocopies of numerous financial statements were dropped off, unfiled. Unfiled papers will be recycled. [Probate Code Section 2620(c) and LRSF 14.34 D. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts.] (7) The proof of service filed on 8/28/23 shows an incomplete title of the pleading on the face of the Notice of Hearing. (8) Notice must also include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 10/9/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MILITINI MAUGATA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/18/2023. The address of Devin Cast listed on the proof of service of the amended petition is different from his address previously reported to the Court. The current address must be confirmed by a verified allegation. In addition, the following issues remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) Absent a satisfaction and release of claim or other proof that Resurgent has agreed to settle for less than the claim amount, the estate cannot be closed until the 90-day period under Prbate Code section 9353 expires. 3) The petitioner's bond must be fixed and filed. See item 5a of the appointment order. 4) Direct notice must be given to Rasela Maugata. CRC 7.51(a)(2). If she has directed the petitioner in writing to give notice in care of her attorney, please file a copy of her written directive.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JEWEL DENT REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED 8/30/17
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Under Probate Code 850 For Order Determining That Certain Real Property, Located In The Cit
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/01/2023, as the following issues remain unaddressed: 1) Each occupant of the 1142 Fitzgerald property identified by the declaration filed on 09/01/2023 must be served in the manner required by Probate Code section 851(a)(2) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq. The proof of service filed on 07/06/2023 shows the occupants were served by mail, which is not a manner authorized by the CCP sections applicable to service of process. If the occupants were served under CCP section 415.30, POS-015 forms signed by the occupants (at least 30 days before the continued hearing) must be filed with a proof of service. If the occupants waived formal service, waivers signed by the occupants must be filed. 2) The addresses of Jewell Hall's 3 children must be confirmed by a verified allegation, as paragraph 14 of the petition did not list them.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MICHAEL J. AND BARBARA A. RUSSELL 2002 TRUST, dated december 27, 2002 as amended and restated
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For (1) Instructions To Trustee, (2) To Enjoin Sale Of Trust Property Pending, And (3) For A Temporary Restraining Order
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/24/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Still no proposed order or courtesy copy of the petition has been received by the assigned examiner as noted in the tentative ruling continuing the 7/12/23 hearing to this date. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B, and 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing appears to have been filed yet for the petition. The proof of service is on the reverse side of the form; if a pleading form of proof of service is filed, a copy of the Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to it. (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (4) It is unclear from a cursory skim of the petition online if all vested or contingent beneficiaries of the trust were listed in the petition. [CRC 7.902.} The proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and proof of service of notice of the petition, showing that at least 30 days' notice was given for the hearing on 10/24/23 (and a courtesy copy of any other new papers) must be received by the assigned examiner by 10/13/23. Only one copy of each document is required to be provided. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

A full copy of the petition must be attached to the supplement. Deadline is 10/9/23 for delivering an endorsed filed courtesy copy of the supplement to the assigned examiner. [Prob. C. Sect. 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARGARET A. FOOTE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/18/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No will or codicil has been lodged with the Court. (2) Item 3g(1)(d) of the petition is incomplete, although William W. Foote is the first nominated executor. (3) Item 5a(2) of the petition is incomplete. (4) Item 8 of the petition is missing both William W. Foote and Julia Bernard, as well as Ross McLauran madden, the second alternate executor named in the codicil. (5) Is Julie Bernard the same person as "Julia" Bernard named in the testamentary documents? (6) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Whose children are the niblings listed in item 8 of the petition? Did the decedent have another brother? Who was he and when did he die? (7) The trustee of the devisee trusts must also be included in item 8. If the petitioner is the trustee, all of the beneficiaries of the trusts must be listed in item 8 of the petition and clearly identified as such. (8) Notice must include the CURRENT language regarding remote appearance procedures. (9) Does the petitioner not have a street address that would confirm item 3h(1) of the petition? (10) Curiously, the proof of publication did not include the attorney's contact information, although the petitioner is represented by an attorney Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 10/9/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
